mnemonics: Think of a tree: BREADTH is how wide the branches spread (number of features), DEPTH is how far down a single root goes (detail of one feature).
keyPoints: Breadth refers to the number of features or functions included in the prototype.
Depth refers to the level of detail and functionality incorporated into a specific feature.
The appropriate balance of depth and breadth is determined by the prototype's specific testing goals.
commonMistakes: Focusing too much on breadth (many features) but with no depth (all are shallow and non-interactive), making it useless for testing user flow.
Adding too much depth (highly detailed single feature) but no breadth (ignoring other key parts of the system), providing an incomplete picture of the user experience.
